Designed to work with all click-on Sonicare models, these brush heads remove up to 20x more plaque than a manual toothbrush while gently improving gum health up to 15x in six weeks (vs. a manual toothbrush). Subscribe & Save on genuine Philips Sonicare brush head replacements and save up to 15%.

reviews.title: Best of the Philips brushes
reviews.text:
I've been using Sonicare for 20 years and I find these A3 heads do the best at cleaning my teeth. The C3 are not too far behind, and the C1 are not far behind those. I just use the old rubbing the tongue on my teeth technique, but with the A3, I seem to go longer before I feel like I need a teeth cleaning at the dentist.
I've also used multiple 3rd party heads and none of them even come close to cleaning like the Philips heads. It's just too bad these things are so expensive, I'll probably stick to the C1 heads unless I see the A3 heads go on sale.

reviews.title: My hygienist noticed a difference!
reviews.text: Used to use the C3s. They are smaller and a bit less comfortable to use than this variant. The main winning point is that my hygienist said "what are you doing differently, your mouth looks way better than usual." I take that as an endorsement, and will continue to use these!

reviews.title: It’s a brush head
reviews.text: It’s a brush head

reviews.title: Works great
reviews.text: These are great toothbrush heads and fit my sonicare perfectly. Cleans teeth well. Would recommend.

reviews.title: Overpriced vs C2
reviews.text: I don’t know what a $15 replacement brush head needs to do to earn 5 stars but this just doesn’t blow me away. I typically buy the C2 replacements, I’ve also used C1 and C3. This is comparable to C3 it’s soft and gives a good clean. I can tell a difference between this and C1 or C2 but when I was using even C1 (which feels harder and I don’t like this one as much C2 is my go to) I didn’t have a cavity and my dentist said I was looking good. I do like that this head tells me when to swap out (C1 doesn’t) but $15 for a replacement is steep considering my toothbrush was $30. I will say after my second root canal I made the switch to a Phillips soniccare and since switching to sonic care from a traditional toothbrush I haven’t had any cavities. So I think using the soniccare with whatever heads you can afford is great for your dental health!

title: mushie Baby Finger Toothbrush (Tradewinds/Stone) 2-Pack
features: Help brushing go smoothly and begin building good habits with our silicone baby finger toothbrushes.
Designed in Sweden and made of soft, food-grade silicone, they slip onto your fingertip so you can gently clean your baby's teeth without irritating gums.
Hand wash in warm water. Replace every 3-4 months.
Caution: Not a toy. Adult supervision required under 3 years of age. Do not leave a child unattended with this product.

reviews.title: LOVE THESE BABY TOOTHBRUSHES!
reviews.text: I love everything from this brand. Mushie has the best baby silicone products and I wish I had bought the other products from Amazon so that I can write these reviews! I've been skeptical about using the cotton baby toothbrushes because I don't want any lint staying in my baby's mouth or it slipping off and be a potential choking hazard. This silicone toothbrush fits perfectly on my finger and makes it so much easier to brush his teeth rather than using a toothbrush and worrying about scraping the roof of his mouth. It's soft all around and has a silicone brush side to brush and a bumpy side for better gum stimulation. It comes in a pack of two and this brand has various modern colors. I highly recommend this product for infant brushing!

reviews.title: Great Product, easy to use!
reviews.text: My baby girl loved this toothbrush! It made brushing her teeth so easy and she loved it! She had so much fun brushing her teeth!

description: Enjoy the unprecedented, professional-like, cleaning experience of all-in-one Multi-Action Solodent SILVER SERIES Toothbrushes. Flossing Slim Ultra Soft Bristles gently clean hard to reach areas, between the teeth, under the gum line, and save tooth enamel from abrasion. They softly stimulate sensitive gums without bleeding or pain. Extra Soft Bristles make this toothbrush perfect for those with gum recession, sensitive teeth, exposed roots, implants and braces. Silver infused embossed PBT bristles naturally fight and minimize bacterial growth on bristles and keep them clean between uses. Innovative Patented 3D Embossed PBT Bristles are designed to gently and effectively remove maximum plaque due to multitouch contacts to tooth surfaces. Actual Gold infused bristles. PBT (polybutylene terephthalate) bristles are more durable and less water-absorbent than traditional nylon bristles and outlast them by 50%. Beautiful patent pending diamond pattern design and ergonomic handle. Designed by a dentist. Made in South Korea.

reviews.title: Edit: Good for my 2 year old
reviews.text:
I realize it says it's a kids toothbrush but it's huge! It's bigger than a regular sized brush for adults. I intended this for my 2 year old for Christmas. It's my fault for not looking at details. I've been searching for an Olaf one and this was on sale. I have not tried it but will update when we do...

Update: I gave this to my daughter early because she saw Olaf. It does fit in her mouth and the on/off buttons are easy for her to push. It's her favorite color, purple. She actually brushes her teeth. Well the toothbrush does the work! I don't see a timer on it. So far, the battery is doing fine. My biggest concern is replacing the head. It's already looking like I should replace.

reviews.title: Clean Teeth &Gums
reviews.text: These toothbrushes are designed to clean teeth while simultaneous removing plaque from the gum line. The outer bristles angle out. I found the brush to be effective. The bristles are gentle. I have sensitive gums and they were not irritated or sore after use. Good brush.

reviews.title: Very wide
reviews.text: This is a good toothbrush. It is quite unusual. It is very, very wide. Make sure you see how much. I don't think I've ever seen one this wide. How it works for you is just going to depend on whether you like that. You can get a lot more at the same time, but some people may find it uncomfortable, especially if you have a small mouth. But it is a way to be very efficient and get a lot of areas. It feels well made. It seems like a quality brush.

reviews.title: Nothing fancy but does the job.
reviews.text: Toothbrushes are necessary and seem to be the one thing that I forget to purchase when I go to the store. So, ordering these Ooak toothbrushes was a no-brainer. These are not fancy. They are traditional toothbrushes that leave your teeth feeling smooth and clean (grit free). There are five rows of soft bristles…the three rows in the center are straight and the outside (colored) ones are slightly angled. The colors of the brushes are neutral, white and orange. The rubber grip handle makes it easy to grip and hold. And finally, on Amazon this two pack is under $7, which is reasonable when compared to other two packs on the market. I’d recommend these.

reviews.title: A unique set of toothbrushes
reviews.text: This toothbrush set has been designed in a V design for the bristles - which means that it looks different than other toothbrushes. The bristles have a lot of space to get in the gum line, in between teeth and works well. I got the medium bristles which are fine with me. I don't want too hard of a bristle, as that is not recommended by the dentist. This does a decent job cleaning.

reviews.title: No stand
reviews.text: I wish it had a stand to hold it upright. The toothbrush quit working. Waste of money

reviews.title: Battery life is NOT what it claims
reviews.text: I've been trying to give this toothbrush the benefit of the doubt, but I give up. I charge it, and for 15 seconds it runs full strength, then slowly declines like my older brush was doing, not holding a charge. Not sure when this was manufactured, or how old the battery is, but it's not holding a charge. It will not run for 365 days on one charge, that's for sure. I will have to return.

reviews.title: Worth every cent
reviews.text: Works wonderfully . Especially for the price

reviews.title: 8 Heads more important than you might think
reviews.text:
I've been trying a bunch of similar toothbrushes lately. First off, most of the brands do not have interchangeable heads, so many of these will end up getting thrown out once the heads are used. For that reason, getting 8 heads with this is an important feature, because at least for the current price, you could hardly buy 8 disposable brushes.

One of the things I like about some others is when they have an induction charger that also acts like a little dock (of those, I prefer the ones with a USB plug rather than an AC plug). Those normally charge a premium, but this one is inexpensive, so at least they have that going for it. It's not like you have to charge it often (although definitely more than once a year), but laying a toothbrush down to plug it in is cumbersome, and it takes a while to charge.

I don't want or need the 30/120 second timers, but every single one I've ever seen (of the "sonic" type) has had that, so how can I complain? I end up brushing for 6-10 minutes most of the time.

Not a bad version of the less expensive off-brand options, but I don't think anyone can predict the future as to if and for how long replacement heads will be available.

reviews.title: Noisy
reviews.text: This toothbrush is fine. It cleans teeth well and is a reasonable price. It comes with 4 toothbrush heads (2 are in the little box with the charger). It charges via USB and has to be laid on its side to charge, which is a bit of a bummer especially considering there is no cover for the toothbrush heads. It doesn't arrive charged. It charges in a couple of hours. It's noisier than other electric toothbrushes we've used. You hear a constant beeping while you brush. Overall, it's okay, but I would recommend a different product if price isn't an issue.

reviews.title: Great for me.
reviews.text:
These are great for me! I've gotten better over the years, but I still tend to beat up and flatten toothbrushes by brushing too hard. These might help you to be conscious of that and calm down a little bit, but if you're really bent on brushing too hard, then they'll just give you hand cramps, since now you have to push harder and at an angle. ...hopefully they'll whip you into shape.

Even if you don't have that problem, they're good solid brushes. They seem to last a while and they leave my mouth feeling clean. I like the simpler heads like this, rather than all those weird newfangled things with all the plastic in them.

reviews.title: Nice For Medium Bristles
reviews.text: I like these toothbrushes. I prefer Soft bristles, but sometimes I want a deeper cleaning so the medium bristles are good for that on occasion. The overall design is nice, and I do feel like it keeps you from brushing too hard with the way it can flex and the bristle placement—it’s enough to get your teeth clean and it’s not so harsh and abrasive compared to some other brushes. It’s still a medium brush, so if you’re sensitive, stick with soft.

reviews.title: Pretty good from someone that is a bit of a skeptic on the benefits of sonic toothbrushes
reviews.text:
Review for: Sparx - Electric Toothbrush, Ultrasonic Toothbrush, Cordless Toothbrush Rechargeable with Light Therapy Technology,

Having tried a couple of different sonic toothbrushes, I have not been impressed with them when compared to a true electric toothbrush. This one though, is much closer to making me a convert. It has much more vibration than others that I have tried and it does feel like it is doing more to clean my teeth. I am not sure if the blue light is really making my teeth whiter but if it is, that is a huge plus.

The brush comes with an extra head, a charger and a set of instructions.

reviews.title: Love this
reviews.text: I’ve been using this all week and really like it. I haven’t had to recharge it. It says it stays charged for 15 hours. I love the 3 different settings (clean, whiten, polish)! My teeth feel cleaner and smoother. It’s a great product!! Highly recommend!

reviews.title: my teeth feels very clean after 1 week of use
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:d99e4c11dd05411b9b597ceb102b22f]] Update: For some reason mine stopped turning on even after I fully charged it. However, customer service was excellent! I reached out to them on Amazon and they immediately sent me a replacement which arrived in 2 days. I am very happy with their level of customer service, in addition to this still being one of the best electronic toothbrush I've used. It really just makes my mouth feel so clean, more than the Philips one ever did. The replacement set that I received has one toothbrush head that emits blue light, and one that emits red light. In my previous set there were only blue light heads so I'm happy I finally get to try the red light. I am currently using this with a whitening gel paste from another brand and it works wonderfully. I am waiting for Sparx to come out with toothpaste (fluoride free please!) and whitening paste. Bottom line: great customer service, great product, and still highly recommend Sparx!

I really like this toothbrush and it really makes me want to brush my teeth more frequently. I have to admit I sometimes get lazy to brush my teeth but this one makes me look forward to my night time routine. I already have an electric toothbrush at home but this one caught my eye because of the LED blue light. I just had to get it because I was curious about this feature.

In terms of the brush power, I think it is great. There are different modes - clean, white, polish, and gum care. Each one vibrates in a different manner. I like that the button is big because if I want to change modes mid brush, my thumb can find it easily. I've used other electric toothbrushes where the power button is so small I couldn't really find it with my thumb. The overall design and size of the button, toothbrush head, and body is perfect, at least for me.

This does use a proprietary brush head, which currently comes in a 1 or 2 pack. It is cheaper to get the 2 pack. I am currently using the blue light version. I believe if you want the red light you'll have to purchase a different brush head. The set I got is both blue light brush heads, so there were two replaceable brush heads in the box. I believe some of their other sets comes with one blue and one red. There is a small charging stand which will need to be plugged into an outlet for the toothbrush to charge.

My only confusion with this product is how the blue light works. Will it work with my own toothpaste or does it require Sparx branded toothpaste? Does the light works to whiten teeth regardless if you use toothpaste or not? Is the LED blue light safe to use on skin? That is the only part that I needed more clarification on, and wished there was more information on this Amazon page.

Regardless of my questions, I still think this is a great electric toothbrush with a lot of potential. My teeth feels so clean after use.

reviews.title: Good Sonic Toothbrush, Confusing Instructions on Whitening
reviews.text: This is an expensive sonic toothbrush that has a blue light feature.  As a sonic toothbrush it works well but there are other options out there that are less expensive that don't require specialized replacement heads.  It is nice that this charges using an USB interface so that saves on cords when traveling.  It is generally not clear how the blue light is supposed to help.  Looking up teeth whitening technology it appears you need a special gel for the blue light to activate teeth whitening.  This is not included with the toothbrush nor discussed in the literature that I can find.  Overall, this works well as a sonic toothbrush, but you will need to purchase special teeth whitening gel to gain the benefits of the blue light as I don't really see a difference by itself.
